commit.message:
fix: 禁用兼容模式的 QUIC/HTTP3 避免 UDP 443 端口冲突

mitmproxy 10.2.4 默认启用 http3=True，在 reverse 模式下会同时绑定
TCP 443 和 UDP 443。当 UDP 443 被其他程序（如 Windows QUIC 服务）
占用时导致启动失败。游戏流量不使用 QUIC，禁用即可。

commit.message:
Fix bad-string-token to return null from get_token_value() (#226)

Per the [CSS tokenization
spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/css-syntax-3/#tokenization),
`bad-string-token` carries no associated data.
`CSSProcessor::get_token_value()` was incorrectly returning the partial
string content up to the breaking codepoint instead of `null`.

Fixes #225.

## Changes

- **`CSS/class-cssprocessor.php`**: Splits `TOKEN_BAD_STRING` out of the
shared `TOKEN_STRING`/`TOKEN_URL` case in `get_token_value()`, giving it
a dedicated case that returns `null`. Also removes a no-op
self-assignment (`$this->token_value = $this->token_value`).
- **`Tests/CSSProcessorTest.php`**: Adds
`test_bad_string_token_value_is_null()` covering the exact scenario from
the issue report.

```php
$p = CSSProcessor::create( "'str\ning'" );
$p->next_token();
// Before: returned 'str' — After: returns null
assert( $p->get_token_type() === CSSProcessor::TOKEN_BAD_STRING );
assert( $p->get_token_value() === null );
```

fix: prevent string concatenation in insurance value totals, fix currency format

purchase_price and estimated_value come from the API as strings.
Using them directly in reduce() caused + to concatenate instead of add,
producing values like $00799.992499.00...

Fix: wrap all values in Number() before arithmetic.
Also use toLocaleString('en-US', {minimumFractionDigits: 2}) across all
display sites to ensure consistent $1,234.56 formatting.

commit.message:
fix: prevent optimize/group from undoing dedup merges (v1.14.2)

The dedup step merges synonymous parents (e.g. Karkki → Makeiset), but
the subsequent optimize/group step could undo this by recreating deleted
parents when Gemini suggested the merged-away name.

Now _deduplicate_parent_products() returns a redirect map of merged-away
names → canonical names. Both _ai_optimize_products() and
_ai_group_products() use this map to redirect any Gemini-suggested group
name to the canonical parent, preventing recreation of deleted parents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Storage Inspector (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, IndexedDB)

Adds full read/write/clear access to all browser storage areas for the
current tab  useful for seeding test state, inspecting authorigin
tokens, and resetting app state between test runs.

Types (packages/shared/src/browser.ts):
  - StorageArea ("local" | "session")
  - StorageEntry { key, value, bytes }
  - CookieEntry { name, value, domain, path, expires, httpOnly, secure, sameSite, size }
  - IndexedDbObjectStore { name, keyPath, autoIncrement, count, rows[] }
  - IndexedDbDatabase { name, version, objectStores }
  - StorageReport { localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, indexedDb, totalBytes }

TabManager (apps/desktop/src/main/tab-manager.ts):
  - captureStorage(): full snapshot via Runtime.evaluate + Network.getCookies
  - getStorage(area, key?): read all or single entry from local/session
  - setStorage(area, entries): batch write to local/session storage
  - clearStorage(area, keys?): remove specific keys or clear entire area
  - setCookie(): upsert via Network.setCookie
  - deleteCookie(name, url?): remove by name
  - clearCookies(url?): delete all cookies for the origin

REST (apps/desktop/src/main/agent-server.ts):
  - GET/POST/DELETE /api/tabs/:id/storage, /storage/local, /storage/session
  - GET/POST/DELETE /api/tabs/:id/cookies, /cookies/:name

SDK (packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts):
  - captureStorage, getStorage, setStorage, clearStorage
  - getCookies, setCookie, deleteCookie, clearCookies
  - delete() helper now accepts optional body
  - Re-exports all new types

Docs (docs/dev-team-features.md):

commit.message:
fix(worker): split Docker log chunks into lines for marker parsing

Docker container.logs(stream=True) can batch multiple lines into a
single chunk. The old code treated each chunk as one 'line', so
CODIFY_THINKING/CODIFY_TOOL_USE_START/CODIFY_ASSISTANT_TEXT markers
embedded within multi-line chunks were never matched by startswith().

Now each chunk is split via splitlines() before processing, ensuring
every CODIFY marker is detected regardless of Docker's buffering.

This fixes the empty Event Stream tab in task detail view.

fix: cancel periodic status check task when process raises ConstraintError (#228)

After a process fails (e.g. via `ConstraintError`), the
`_periodic_status_check` background task on waiting components was not
being cancelled — causing it to log `"State backend not connected,
skipping status check"` every 20s indefinitely after process teardown.

# Summary

Python's `asyncio.wait()` does **not** propagate cancellation to its
child tasks when the outer task is cancelled. When
`LocalProcess.run()`'s `TaskGroup` cancelled component B's task due to
component A raising `ConstraintError`, the `CancelledError` raised in
`_io_read_with_status_check` left the `_periodic_status_check` task
orphaned in the event loop.

# Changes

- **`plugboard/component/component.py`**: In
`_io_read_with_status_check`, wrap `asyncio.wait()` in `try/except
BaseException` and explicitly cancel `status_task` before re-raising.
`io_task` is intentionally left uncancelled — cancelling it leaves stale
entries in `IOController._read_tasks` that break subsequent reads.

```python
# Before
done, pending = await asyncio.wait(
    (
        asyncio.create_task(self._periodic_status_check()),
        asyncio.create_task(self.io.read(timeout=read_timeout)),
    ),
    return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED,
)

# After
status_task = asyncio.create_task(self._periodic_status_check())
io_task = asyncio.create_task(self.io.read(timeout=read_timeout))
try:
    done, pending = await asyncio.wait(
        (status_task, io_task),
        return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED,
    )
except BaseException:
    status_task.cancel()
    raise
```

- **`tests/integration/test_process_with_components_run.py`**: Added
`test_constraint_error_stops_background_status_check` — patches
`IO_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` to 0.1s, runs a process where the producer
raises `ConstraintError`, and asserts the consumer's background status
check task count does not increase after the process fails.

commit.message:
fix: add root vercel.json for monorepo safety + document Vercel incident

Root package.json was added for Railway/Railpack but Vercel had no matching
root-level config. When Vercel points at the repo root (not frontend/), it
cannot detect Next.js or find the .next output directory.

Fix:
- Add root vercel.json with explicit framework, installCommand, buildCommand,
  outputDirectory, and security headers for the repo-root deploy case
- frontend/vercel.json remains the preferred path when Vercel dashboard is
  set to Root Directory = frontend

Also document both Vercel incidents in GUIDE.md troubleshooting section.

Phase 3 proactive meal prediction results (EXP-1121–1128)

Key findings:
- EXP-1121: Proactive AUC=0.806 (no net_flux), glucose adds +0.164
- EXP-1122: Multi-harmonic time features useless (-0.004)
- EXP-1123: Parametric hazard models worse than GBT (0.536 vs 0.642)
- EXP-1124: History window features hurt (-0.016)
- EXP-1125: PRE-MEAL GLUCOSE SIGNATURES breakthrough — AUC=0.840
  combined, +0.198 over time-only. Best single discovery of campaign.
- EXP-1126: Ensemble 1/11 meets strict target (needs 1125 features)
- EXP-1127: Honest proactive AUC=0.767, lead time=17min, frac30=15%
- EXP-1128: Production baseline: 23.7 alerts/day, PPV=0.24 (needs work)

Fix: polyfit unpacking bug in EXP-1125 glucose slope computation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CWE v4.19.1 per-domain security audit taxonomies (#230)

Ingest the MITRE CWE v4.19.1 corpus (969 weaknesses, 944 active) and
generate 13 per-domain taxonomy files for PromptKit security audits.

Artifacts:
- scripts/ingest-cwe.py: standalone, reusable ingestion script
- data/cwe/4.19.1/: normalized data, domain mappings, metadata
- taxonomies/cwe-*.md: 13 domain taxonomy files (626 mapped CWEs)

Domains: kernel-mode-c-cpp (113), native-user-mode-c-cpp (172),
managed-dotnet (92), web-js-ts (265), web-backend (423),
cloud-service (113), iac (8), firmware-embedded (226),
crypto-protocols (63), data-processing (69), cli-tools (65),
mobile-app (23), container-k8s (5).

Mapping uses 4-priority algorithm: CWE view membership, platform
match, context/keyword analysis, and override rules. All domain
exclusion and consistency checks pass.

Template integration (domain parameter support) tracked in Issue #228.

Co-authored-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>
